title: Use of sex appeal and fanservice justified
display_title: Use of sex appeal and fanservice justified
wikitext:
[]: Use of sex appeal and fanservice justified             Quote Post by Korgan » Wed May 25, 2011 9:06 am "Serious" opinions on Eva, especially mainstream ones, have long looked down on its fanservice-y moments. Y'know, Shinji falling on Rei, Asuka explaining "thermal expansion", etc. However, I believe that, aside from there being nothing wrong with a little eye candy in any series' lighter moments, this is used deliberately to share a bit of the characters' mindset with the viewer. Like, Shinji and Asuka are frustrated teens, and sex is obviously never far from their minds unless shit's going too horribly wrong at the moment. The male viewer would catch himself staring at the female cast, especially when they're wearing something revealing, but so does Shinji. Misato is using sex as a substitute for honest emotional contact, so she gets all the three actual scenes (2 in the series and 1 in EoE, unless I'm forgetting something). Overall, the erotic tension is just huge throughout, and this adds to the dramatic, emotionally charged atmosphere. Without all such content, Eva just wouldn't work as well. Any of you had similar thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Wed May 25, 2011 9:08 am Aye, Asuka explaining "thermal expansion" cracked me up too. I just fear that they will go too far.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed May 25, 2011 9:20 am Check out these two threads, there's some good discussion about it that might answer some of your questions: Fan-service - A Black Mark on Evangelion Is "fanservice" acceptable?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Wed May 25, 2011 9:23 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 25, 2011 10:16 am C#mon, it's not like every single thingt has to be deep or meaningful just because it's in EVA - It has it's comedic moments and a little fun won't kill it if it still has depht in other places. smex is a part of life, ppl aren't always fully clothed... also, all the naked skin creates a certain mood, a notion of, duh, primalness, raw unfiltered humanity, like everything else in the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Wed May 25, 2011 10:28 am Here is my view on "fanservice". Firstly: If nudity makes sense in the shot (the character is in the shower, or sleeps in the nude, or has some valid reason to be naked/scantly clad) then by all means, show tits and ass. If the character's breasts would be giggling naturally from the activity they're doing and they had a valid reason not to wear a bra, show the giggling flesh sacks. If you have to go out of your way at all to show anything. Don't bother. Secondly: If you decide to interject pointless fan service, you commit to it. Either you go for the completely ridiculous route, (the whore in Gun x Sword who piloted her mech via stripper pole and orgasms comes to mind) or you go Game of Thrones/Qwaser on that shit. (I want a completely pointless 10 minute lesbian sex scene thrown in for absolutely no reason).

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Wed May 25, 2011 10:34 am I 'd at rebuild 2.0.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed May 25, 2011 11:39 am The nudity in Neon Genesis Evangelion seems to follow the same progression to the arc as the rest of the story does. At first it's fun and playful, kinda like the rest of the show. You can laugh at it, and relate to it however else you want to. "Yes. Misato is attractive and we know it," we think as the show invites us to watch her get dressed in the morning. But then everything goes to hell, and the nudity in the last half of the series seems to strip the characters away from all barriers and exposes their true decrepit selves. With absolutely nothing to shield herself from the viewer Asuka is left to have her troubling past forced to the fore front of her mind, reopening old wounds that never really healed in the first place and scarring herself yet again, until finally you see her nude in a condemned house with blood leaking from her wrists, then again as being only an unconscious "side-dish" for Shinji. If that's "fun" for the viewer then one of Saddam Hussein's sons must have been watching. Also, like everything else in the show, once you've seen it to completion you can never look back at any of the beginning elements the same way ever again. One starts seeing symbolism of torn families and false attempts at being motherly in Misato's bra, childish naivety in Asuka's panties, and this constant nagging feeling that Rei's body was always meant to be a vessel. EDIT: Tarnsman wrote: If the character's breasts would be giggling naturally from the activity they're doing and they had a valid reason not to wear a bra, show the giggling flesh sacks.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 25, 2011 2:32 pm I mean, it's never been so over the top - what makes most the fanservice in EVA hot (when it isn't disturbing) is that we see them in situation where they have reasons to be scantiy clad, they're not "trying" to act hot (and when they are, it's a plot point.) and that's what gives it its effect, this uh, naturalness.

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Wed May 25, 2011 3:23 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I think you mean "jiggling". "Giggling" boobs brings Chatterbox to mind, which is another "valid" way to get some fan service in there, I guess. Just make the whole movie about a sexual body appendage that can even say a few lines.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Wed May 25, 2011 4:40 pm I think some of the fanservice-y moments in the original series were justified because they tied into Shinji's emerging sexual awareness, and occasionally just because they were funny (e.g. "thermal expansion"); and as FFFE points out, as the series progressed the playful tone of the nudity and sexual situations evolved into a darker, more psychologically intense form, in which nudity came to represent characters' vulnerability, or - as in the case of GNR - purity. But the stuff like the blatantly gratuitous crotch-shots, shower scenes, suggestively-clad teenage girls, etc. - that stuff I always found kind of leery and uncomfortable, (and not the good kind of uncomfortable, like you want,) and it makes the show and the new movies in particular awfully hard to show to someone who isn't an anime fan if you're trying to sell them on the show's formidable artistic merits. It's one reason I've come to be dubious about the disparity in tone between the first and second halves of the series, and also a reason I'm still fairly dubious about Rebuild (of which 2.0 has got to have the most jarringly gratuitous fanservice yet).

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu May 26, 2011 2:41 am Personally I found all the in the next time preview way overselling things, because I was benchmarking against other contemporary material like BurnUp/W+Excess or Dirty Pair Flash . Can't comment about the Rebuilds -- just note that the likely comparands would be things like Strike Witches and Highschool of the Dead .

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu May 26, 2011 7:54 am @ gatotsu911: I agree with most of your points as well. Though, the only fanservice I found unnecessary, gratuitous, and pointless in NME was that one bath scene with Misato in 2.22 . Everything else, even Asuka shower scene, seemed to arc to a point at some time in the film. (Though it was also extremely over-the-top...) I guess if one compares Misato's bath scene in 1.11 to her bath shot in 2.22 one can try and argue some sort of arc in them. But NME is mainly still in that "let's have fun/be bad-ass" stages of story-telling, not introducing anything darker/"deeper" with plot themes, character moments, and nudity motifs until the 10th Angel attack at the end of the movie. So I guess we can only wait and see if any of this has a point at the end of it all. Tarnsman wrote: Nah, everyone knows that breasts can laugh. (Shut up! I'm dyslexic!)

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Thu May 26, 2011 10:18 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: On that note, The Cinema Snob did a review of a movie called Chatterbox . You should watch it. :)

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu May 26, 2011 10:36 am Tarnsman wrote: I think I've seen it. Is it a french movie about a talking vagina?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu May 26, 2011 10:47 am There's a definite contrast within NGE between "fun and lighthearted" and "dark and serious." We see this in particular among character interactions over the course of the series. The use of fanservice also serves to fulfill this contrast, as somebody already pointed out, with later uses of nudity and sexuality. I think it would be a detriment to the quality of the series to remove this point of contrast, since it echoes the bigger paradigm within the character drama. NTE also seems to really be emphasizing this contrast, making it much more apparent than it was in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Thu May 26, 2011 12:59 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Though, the only fanservice I found unnecessary, gratuitous, and pointless in NME was that one bath scene with Misato in 2.22 . Everything else, even Asuka shower scene, seemed to arc to a point at some time in the film.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu May 26, 2011 3:07 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Even Asuka's cameltoe shot/implied hatred of proper shirts and Mari going on about her measurements? In scenes where Shinji wasn't even present?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu May 26, 2011 3:12 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Even Asuka's cameltoe shot

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Thu May 26, 2011 3:15 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Not saying that isn't natural to the character, but it's not really something that seems really important to the plot just yet.
